sense.definition: a type of card game in which players compete to win tricks, which are groups of cards played in a single round according to the rules of the game

examples.x: In a trick-taking game like Bridge, good communication and strategy are key to winning.
She was excited to teach her friends how to play a trick-taking game she had learned on vacation.
The game of Pinochle is a classic trick-taking game with complex rules and scoring.
Trick-taking games like Euchre are popular at family gatherings and friendly competitions.

sense.definition: a card game for 2-6 players in which they try to gather points by collecting sets of cards, using 2 decks of cards and 4 jokers

a card game, usually for two players, who try to score a specific point before the other player and during the game record their points by placing small wooden pieces called pegs into the holes of a board

examples.x: Cribbage is a fun game to play on quiet evenings with friends.
The wooden board used to keep score is one of the unique features of cribbage.
Learning cribbage can take some time, but it becomes easier with practice.
Many people enjoy cribbage for its mix of strategy and luck.
Playing cribbage is a great way to sharpen mental math skills.

sense.definition: a game of cards in which players must gather 10 points in order to win

examples.x: Gin rummy is a fun and competitive card game that is easy to pick up.
They spent the evening playing gin rummy with a few friends.
They decided to play gin rummy while waiting for dinner to be ready.
She won the last round of gin rummy by forming a perfect sequence.

sense.definition: a card game in which two or more players bet on the values of the cards handed to them, often played in order to win money
a game of cards that its players aim to form the right combinations of sequences or sets
a card game in which players place their cards down one by one and the winner is someone who first says 'snap' as soon as noticing two similar cards are put together
a card game that has only one player
a card game that is typically played with a standard deck of 52 cards by three or more players, with the objective of getting rid of all of one's cards by playing them face down in a pile

a simple card game that is typically played by two players with a standard deck of 52 cards, in which the objective is to win all of the cards in the deck by playing higher-ranking cards than one's opponent
a classic card game that is typically played by two to four players with a standard deck of 52 cards, in which the objective is to get rid of all of one's cards by playing them onto a discard pile

a fast-paced card game that is typically played by two or more players with a standard deck of 52 cards, in which the objective is to win all of the cards in the deck by slapping the pile when certain combinations of cards are played

a simple card game that is typically played by two to six players with a standard deck of 52 cards, in which the objective is to collect sets of four cards of the same rank by asking other players for cards of that rank

examples.x: We played Go Fish for hours last night and had so much fun.
I kept asking my brother for a queen, but he kept saying 'Go fish!'
Do you want to play Go Fish with me?
She won the game of Go Fish by collecting all four of the sevens.
In Go Fish, it's important to remember which cards the other players ask for

sense.definition: a single-player card game that is played with a standard deck of 52 cards, in which the objective is to move all of the cards to four foundation piles in ascending order, separated by suit

a solitaire card game that is played with two decks of standard playing cards, with the objective of removing all of the cards from the tableau by creating sequences of cards in descending order, regardless of suit
a popular single-player card game that is played with a standard deck of 52 cards

a fast-paced card game that is typically played by two players with a standard deck of 52 cards, in which the objective is to get rid of all of one's cards by playing them onto a central pile as quickly as possible

a fast-paced card game that is typically played by two or more players with a standard deck of 52 cards, in which the objective is to win all of the cards by being the first player to slap a jack when it is played

examples.x: Slapjack is a great way to test your reflexes while having fun with friends.

sense.definition: a card game where players aim to be the first to get rid of all their cards by playing them in specific sequences or following certain rules

examples.x: Crazy Eights is a popular shedding-type card game where the goal is to get rid of all your cards.
In a shedding-type card game, players must carefully plan their moves to avoid getting stuck with too many cards.
Shedding-type card games are usually fast-paced, with players racing to empty their hands before their opponents.
A good strategy in a shedding-type card game is to pay attention to the cards your opponents are playing.
The excitement in a shedding-type card game increases when only a few cards remain in the players’ hands.

sense.definition: a simple card game that is typically played with a standard deck of 52 cards by two or more players, in which the objective is to avoid being left with the unpaired "Old Maid" card at the end of the game
a popular Austrian card game played with a 20-card deck, where players aim to win tricks and accumulate points through various card combinations

examples.x: We played Herzeln with our friends last night, and it was a great way to spend time together.
I had never heard of Herzeln until I visited Austria, but now I enjoy playing it regularly.
The game of Herzeln uses a 20-card deck, which makes it different from many other card games.
We played a few rounds of Herzeln, and I managed to win by keeping my points low.

sense.definition: a card game played with a special deck where players aim to be the first to discard all their cards by matching them with the top card of the pile

examples.x: My sister is unbeatable at Uno; she always seems to know the right move.
I always get excited when we play Uno, especially when I have a good hand.
He won the game of Uno by using a well-timed Reverse card.
We played several rounds of Uno during the family gathering.

sense.definition: a type of game where players play cards in a continuous, fast-paced manner without taking turns, and the gameplay occurs in real-time, requiring quick thinking and reaction to the changing game state

examples.x: In a real-time card game, players must act quickly and think fast to keep up with the action.
Unlike traditional card games, a real-time card game requires all players to play at the same time.
The excitement of real-time card games comes from the fast-paced nature and constant decisions players must make.
A real-time card game like Spit can get very intense as players try to outpace each other.
Playing a real-time card game often leads to moments of chaos, where players must stay focused to win.

sense.definition: a shedding-type card game where players aim to be the first to get rid of all their cards by playing higher-ranked cards or specific card combinations

examples.x: We played Big Two for hours last weekend and had a lot of fun.
If you cannot play a higher card than the previous one in Big Two, you have to pass.
She managed to win Big Two by playing a straight flush at just the right moment.
Big Two requires a good understanding of card combinations and timing.
We decided to play Big Two while waiting for the others to arrive at the party.

sense.definition: a card game that involves deductive reasoning and is played with a standard deck of cards, where players try to figure out a secret rule or pattern governing the play of cards

examples.x: Playing Eleusis requires careful observation, as the rule changes with each round.
If you play a card that doesn’t fit the rule in Eleusis, you must draw a penalty card.
I enjoy playing Eleusis because it challenges my ability to think logically and pay attention to patterns.
When playing Eleusis, it is important to remember the cards played to spot the hidden rule.

sense.definition: a two-player card game played with multiple decks of cards, where players aim to be the first to play all their cards from their individual stockpiles to build ascending piles in the center while blocking their opponent's moves

examples.x: She quickly figured out the best way to win at Spite and Malice by blocking her opponent’s moves.
I enjoy playing Spite and Malice with my friends because it’s fast-paced and fun.
The game of Spite and Malice involves a lot of careful planning and observation.
He was so focused on winning that he didn’t realize he had given me the chance to play my last card in Spite and Malice.

sense.definition: a card game played with a standard deck of cards, where players aim to have the lowest score by forming specific card combinations and attempting to get rid of high-value cards

a unique card game with unconventional rules and secretive nature, where players must deduce and learn the rules as they play, often resulting in unexpected penalties for breaking unknown rules or making mistakes during the game

an Italian card game played with a standard deck of cards, where players aim to capture specific cards on the table by matching their values with cards from their hand and earn points for combinations of cards captured

examples.x: We played a few rounds of Scopa after dinner last night.
Scopa is a great game to enjoy with family and friends.
During the vacation, we spent hours playing Scopa on the beach.
She was very good at Scopa and managed to capture almost all the cards.
After learning the rules of Scopa, I quickly became competitive in the game.

sense.definition: a fast-paced card game where players try to collect four-of-a-kind cards and quickly grab a spoon from the center of the table, leading to a fun and competitive race

examples.x: In the game of Spoons, players quickly pass cards around the table while trying to collect four of a kind.
She was so quick in Spoons that she grabbed a spoon before anyone else noticed.
The kids played Spoons all afternoon, and the game never got old because of how fast-paced it was.
We played Spoons for hours last night and laughed the whole time.
The game of Spoons can get pretty chaotic as players try to grab spoons without anyone noticing.

sense.definition: a strategic card game typically played with a standard deck of cards, where players aim to form specific card combinations and avoid exceeding a certain total point value in their hand

examples.x: The goal of Yaniv is to have the lowest total value of cards in hand.
In Yaniv, players take turns drawing and discarding cards to reduce the value of their hands.
They played several rounds of Yaniv, with each player trying to outsmart the others by managing their cards wisely.
She won the round of Yaniv, thanks to her quick thinking and perfect timing in calling it.

sense.definition: a two-player solitaire card game played with a standard deck of cards, where players try to move their cards onto common foundation piles by building up in suit and sequence

examples.x: We spent the afternoon playing Russian Bank, and I finally won after a tough game.
Russian Bank is one of my favorite two-player card games because it’s fast-paced and strategic.
It took a few rounds to understand the rules of Russian Bank, but once I got the hang of it, I couldn’t stop playing.
She quickly cleared her pile of cards in Russian Bank, leaving me with almost no moves.
We played several games of Russian Bank, and each time, the match ended with a close call.

sense.definition: a card game played with a standard deck of cards where players aim to be the first to get rid of their cards by playing higher-ranked cards or specific card combinations

examples.x: The game of Thirteen is a fun way to pass the time with friends.
In Thirteen, players must think carefully about when to play their best cards.
She was the first to play all her cards and won the round of Thirteen.
I lost the game of Thirteen because I couldn’t play a higher card when my turn came.
We played Thirteen for hours last night, and I finally figured out the best strategy.

sense.definition: a card game played with a standard deck of cards by two to four players, where players capture cards on the table by matching cards from their hand with the same value
a traditional Japanese card game that involves players listening to poetry readings and quickly identifying and capturing matching cards based on the poems' texts or first lines
a traditional Japanese card game played with a deck of hanafuda cards, involving matching cards based on their pictures and suits to form specific combinations
a type of card game that includes multiple games played with a single deck of cards, offering a variety of different gameplay experiences within one set

examples.x: He brought out a compendium card game during the road trip, so everyone could play something different.
We decided to try the compendium card game because we wanted something that could keep everyone entertained.
A compendium card game is perfect for parties because it offers a wide variety of options for different group sizes.

sense.definition: a card game that is typically played with a standard deck of 52 cards by two or more players, in which the objective is to get rid of all of one's cards by playing them face down, while bluffing about the cards being played
